name: Baby Ruth Layer Cake
recipeIngredient: 8 Baby Ruth bars (1.9oz), cut into pieces
For the Baby Ruth Filling & Topping:
6 Baby Ruth bars (1.9oz), cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the cut-up Baby Ruth bars, peanut butter, and evaporated milk in the top of a double boiler. Heat and stir until melted and blended.
2. Cool the mixture.
3. In a bowl, combine flour, baking soda, and baking powder; mix well.
4. Cream butter and sugar thoroughly. Add eggs and beat until light and fluffy. Blend in the cooled candy bar mixture and ½ tsp vanilla.
5. Add dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk, blending on low speed after each addition until just combined.
6. Pour batter into two buttered and floured 9-inch round layer pans.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for about 30 minutes.
8. Cool in the pans on a rack for 10 minutes. Turn out onto a rack and finish cooling.
Baby Ruth Filling & Topping:
1. Melt candy bars in the top of a double boiler.
2. In a saucepan, combine egg yolks, evaporated milk, sugar, and butter. Cook and stir over medium heat until thickened, about 10 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and blend in melted candy bars, coconut, and peanuts.
4. Cool, then beat vigorously with a wooden spoon.
